gaps.title: Add @woven_animation trait for active weaving process on tapestry objects (A-009 example-driven request)
gaps.description: While composing the Rune-Loom of the Sigil Weavers stress-test (rune-loom-sigil-weavers-*-2026-05-18.holo), the scene required expressing a tapestry being actively woven — thread advancing row by row with visible shuttle movement, growing completion, and warp thread tension. The existing @tapestry trait handles cloth physics and drape simulation but has no concept of the weaving process itself. Proposed API: @woven_animation { weft_speed_mm_per_s, completion_pct, shuttle_visible, warp_tension_variance, advance_direction: 'bottom_up' | 'top_down', thread_material_override }. This would enable procedural 'in-progress artwork' scenes without custom animation scripting.
